### What is the idea?

command `conda compare` which was introduced in 2016 with #3781 not written in the docs. I searched the docs and no information was there. I was only able to see the command in github issues and the release notes, but not as a documented text.

### Why is this needed

Every feature should be in the docs. `conda compare` certainly has some use cases.

### What should happen?

It should be present in the docs

- /NoRegistry

  - https://github.com/conda/conda/issues/10585
- /NoShortcuts
  - https://github.com/conda/conda/issues/7507

Checklist

- [X] I added a descriptive title
- [X] I searched for other feature requests and couldn't find a duplicate (including also the ``type-feature`` tag)
- [X] I confirmed that it's not related to another project (see the above section)

### What is the idea?

If the user directory is `C:/Users/SomeUserName` then conda is installed properly.
If the user directory is `C:/Users/User木` then conda is not installed at all.
This is because conda still prevents non-ascii user directory names because it says some packages do not support non-ascii user directory names. However, since Python 3.0 all strings are interpreted as unicode and the case where the package makes error due to the folder structure is rare. Shouldn't conda be installed even if the user directory contains non-ascii characters?

### Why is this needed

Non-alphabetical users usually have user directory names with non-ascii characters. This is why it's needed.

### What should happen?

Non-alphabetical users should be able to install conda on their machine(not as allusers)

### Additional Context
